entries.summary: Two Indian soldiers and four suspected rebels killed in Kulgam district gunfights.
Suspected rebels ambushed army vehicle in Kathua, killing five Indian soldiers.
Four Indian soldiers killed during firefight with suspected rebels in Doda district forests.
One Indian soldier and a suspected infiltrator killed near Kupwara along Line of Control.
Gunmen killed 26 tourists at Pahalgam meadow, mainly Hindu men, witnesses said.
Kashmir Resistance front claimed responsibility; Indian security forces launched manhunt across valley.
Pakistan announced retaliatory measures including airspace closure and trade suspension with India.
Indian forces began demolishing family homes of suspected Kashmiri militants across valley.
India and Pakistan exchanged small-arms fire along Line of Control for second night.
Troops traded fire across Kashmir border for third consecutive day.
Kashmiri residents told reporters everyone now lives in fear after Pahalgam fallout.
Operation Sindoor strikes hit nine sites in Pakistan-administered Kashmir and Pakistan.
India and Pakistan agreed to ceasefire after four days of cross-border fighting.
Kashmiris voiced fear and fatigue after ceasefire, demanding a permanent solution.
Report alleged separatist leader Yasin Malik served as Indian intelligence asset for years.
Pakistani spouses expelled after attack remained separated from Indian families months later.
Voting began in Kashmir's first assembly elections since India revoked territory's autonomy.
Gunmen opened fire on tourists in Pahalgam, killing 26 in worst attack since 2000.
Modi cut foreign trip short, vowed to pursue attackers to ends of earth.
India suspended Indus Waters Treaty and expelled Pakistani diplomats after deadly attack.
Indian troops blew up family homes of two suspects; exchanged fire across LoC.
Indian crackdown intensified in Kashmir with further home demolitions linked to militants.
More than 780 Pakistani nationals left India by deportation deadline, including Kashmir spouses.
India launched Operation Sindoor missile strikes on nine sites; seven killed in Kashmir.
International community urged restraint as India-Pakistan strikes continued across border.
Overnight fighting in Kashmir shook the freshly announced India-Pakistan ceasefire.
Ceasefire largely held as both sides claimed victory in brief conflict.
Mixed Indian-Pakistani families in Kashmir feared permanent separation after mass deportations.
Indian security forces said they killed three militants behind Pahalgam tourist massacre.
Home minister addressed parliament debate without acknowledging intelligence shortcomings, opposition said.
Rights groups reported India expelled over 1,500 Muslims since the April attack.
